articles.title: Why U.S. Drug Shortages Are a National Security Issue
articles.summary: Why chronic generic-drug shortages are not just a healthcare problem but a supply-chain and national-security one, and where regulatory professionals fit into strengthening U.S. pharmaceutical resilience.

articles.title: What FDA's AI Guidance Actually Means for Regulatory Submissions, and Why Most of Us Aren't Ready
articles.summary: A practical read on FDA's emerging AI guidance and what it means, concretely, for the quality and review of regulatory submissions, cutting through the hype to the implications for everyday RA work.

articles.title: The Module 3 Deficiencies That Most Often Delay ANDA Approval, and How to Catch Them Before You File
articles.summary: A practical, manufacturer-side guide to the Module 3 (CMC) deficiencies that most often send ANDAs back for another FDA review cycle, and how to catch them before filing.

articles.title: Regulatory Bottlenecks in ANDA Approvals: Submission Quality and Generic Drug Shortages, Lessons from ICH-Harmonized Markets
articles.summary: Argues that a large share of ANDA approval delay comes from preventable submission-quality deficiencies, not the underlying science, and shows how global ICH-harmonized CTD experience can be applied to accelerate FDA-aligned generic supply chains.

articles.title: Real-World Evidence in Pharmaceutical Regulation: Administrative and Policy Challenges in Post-Market Drug Safety Surveillance
articles.summary: Introduces an original four-challenges framework (data governance, health-IT infrastructure, workforce capacity, and layered privacy compliance) for healthcare administrators integrating real-world evidence into post-market drug-safety surveillance across the U.S., Canada, and the EU.
